rows.description: Summa Solutorum et Receptorum pro hoc anno 2do die Novembris 1855
rows.notes: Page title at top of page. All words clearly legible and there are no monetary figures on this line.
Numbers in the three right-hand columns align well under the ruling lines. Pounds (963) and shillings (17) are clear; pence shows a small loop indicating a halfpenny (interpreted as 0.5).

Pounds read as '315' (three clear digits). Shillings '15' and pence '7' align with column lines. Minor uncertainty from slightly faded ink on the first digit but context (subtotal) supports this reading.
Pounds '3614' clearly written; shillings '16' and pence '1' are well aligned. Ink and alignment are clear for this line.

Pounds (669) and shillings (4) are clear. Pence shows '1' with a small tail that looks like a quarter (interpreted as 0.25). Slight uncertainty on the fraction mark but position and shape favor a farthing (1/4).
Subtotal line; numbers are written heavier/darker and align exactly under the £/s/d column rulings. Reads clearly as 5563 pounds, 12 shillings, 11 3/4 pence (0.75).

rows.description: Soluta viz: pro Rectoria Schol: et Off:
rows.notes: Description handwriting slightly stylized but legible as 'Soluta viz...' Amount columns align; pounds 508, shillings 16, pence 8 are clearly formed though the description letters are somewhat flourished.
Short description 'Ordinarie' is clear. Numeric columns are unambiguous: 657 lbs, 11 s, 6 d, all within valid ranges and aligned.
Pounds '4229' and pence '10' are clear; shillings column contains '0'. Alignment to ruling lines is good and values pass validation.

Subtotal of Soluta. Heavy/dark numerals align perfectly in £/s/d columns reading 5395 pounds, 9 shillings, 0 pence. Calculation checks with the three lines above (508+657+4229 -> yields 5395; shillings and pence carry-over consistent).
This is the carried-in total from the Recepta column. Values identical to the earlier 'Summa Receptorum' (5563 12 11 3/4). Clear alignment and heavy ink make this unambiguous.
Deduction line (appears with a leading dash in the manuscript). Matches the 'Summa Solutorum' above. Numerals are bold and precisely aligned; no ambiguity.
Balance (Ex Receptis minus Deductis Solutis). Numerals are bold and exactly aligned: 168 pounds, 3 shillings, 11 3/4 pence. Arithmetic checks with the two totals above.
Section heading introducing the signatories. No monetary amounts on this line and lettering is clear.

Signature-style line. The name 'J. P. Lightfoot' is readable; the word following is in stylized script and ambiguous (interpreted as 'Reutoren' / 'Rectorum'). No amounts present; handwriting flourish reduces confidence.

First and last name letters are legible as 'G. H. Curtis'. The word after the dash looks like 'Auditorum' or 'Auiditorum' (some letters faded/curled). No amounts; moderate uncertainty about the exact post/title spelling.
Given name abbreviated as 'Guil.' (common abbreviation for William) and surname read as 'Andrews'. The trailing word resembles 'Socium' (colleague). Handwritten, somewhat faint; no monetary amounts.
Name appears as 'Jos. N. Tweed' with a following title that resembles 'Dean'. Ink is lighter here but the letters are mostly legible; no amounts present.

Handwritten signature block. First name abbreviation 'Henr.' (Henry) and surname 'Lowe' are plausible; trailing words are faded and heavily stylized ('Bursarum Seniorum' is a best-reading). No amounts present and several letters are uncertain.
Surname reads as 'Ince' with abbreviated given name 'Gal.' (Gal. likely Galfridus/Gale). Trailing 'Socium' or 'Socium.' is plausible. Ink is lighter and stylized; no monetary data.
